subject: [DCAEGEN2] Add new OpenAPI schemas configmap
commit_message:
[DCAEGEN2] Add new OpenAPI schemas configmap

- Added ConfigMap with 3gpp SA91-Rel16 OpenAPI schemas
- Moved ConfigMaps from 'dcaegen2' chart to 'dcaegen2-services'
- Removed old SA88-Rel16 OpenAPI schemas

Signed-off-by: Michal Banka <michal.banka@nokia.com>
Change-Id: Ibae0786c7d5e498aa076186a580a268913a57692
Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2690

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Why are 3gpp SA91-Rel16 files being added?  Is there a corresponding REQ/EPIC requiring/tracking this additions for Istanbul? This list will need to be reviewed closer by VES/VNFREQ Team and only files relevant from ONAP default processing must be included.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> Why are 3gpp SA91-Rel16 files being added?  Is there a corresponding REQ/EPIC requiring/tracking this additions for Istanbul? This list will need to be reviewed closer by VES/VNFREQ Team and only files relevant from ONAP default processing must be included.

This a new release of 3GPP, which solves a number of bugs, which were present in previous versions.
This is still rel-16, so no new functionality is expected here, only BugFixes.

With this release, 3GPP has actually restructured the files - e.g. there are new "comDefs.yaml" files which contain definitions of common objects used in different management services.
This is why the amount of files is different than previously, but essentially the functionality is the same.

We do not have an intention to support more, than what was present previously in Honolulu release.
It is simply, that 3GP is restructuring the files, to simplify the definitions and make them more manageable. 

Still, it is absolutely fine to review the files, and if there are any files, which shall not be present (I think, I`ll ask about some of them), then we shall remove them.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(3 comments)

Please check, if these 3 files are really needed for Fault, Performance Monitoring, Provisioning.
Uploaded patch set 2.

Patch Set 5: Code-Review+1

(4 comments)

Ok, I give my +1 on this change.
As I wrote before, the strucutre of files has been changed, and certain common definitions have been brought to different files in 3GPP openAPI definitions. That`s why we don`t have 4 files anymore, we`ll have more to cover the same functionality actually.

As this is a bug-fix related to 3GPP openAPIs for release 16 (and not release 17...), I think, we shall be good to merge this one, after verify jobs / gating jobs are succeeding.

> Patch Set 5: Code-Review+1
> 
> (4 comments)
> 
> Ok, I give my +1 on this change.
> As I wrote before, the strucutre of files has been changed, and certain common definitions have been brought to different files in 3GPP openAPI definitions. That`s why we don`t have 4 files anymore, we`ll have more to cover the same functionality actually.
> 
> As this is a bug-fix related to 3GPP openAPIs for release 16 (and not release 17...), I think, we shall be good to merge this one, after verify jobs / gating jobs are succeeding.

Damian - Thanks for clarification. One question - as these updates on same rls 16, can the old files (sa88-rel16) be removed? Trying to understand the need for supporting both versions.

Patch Set 6: Code-Review+1

> Patch Set 6:
> 
> > Patch Set 5: Code-Review+1
> > 
> > (4 comments)
> > 
> > Ok, I give my +1 on this change.
> > As I wrote before, the strucutre of files has been changed, and certain common definitions have been brought to different files in 3GPP openAPI definitions. That`s why we don`t have 4 files anymore, we`ll have more to cover the same functionality actually.
> > 
> > As this is a bug-fix related to 3GPP openAPIs for release 16 (and not release 17...), I think, we shall be good to merge this one, after verify jobs / gating jobs are succeeding.
> 
> Damian - Thanks for clarification. One question - as these updates on same rls 16, can the old files (sa88-rel16) be removed? Trying to understand the need for supporting both versions.

From my perspective, we can remove the older SA88-rel16 files, this is perfectly fine with me.
Still, they might be people, who built something around the oler SA88-rel16 descritpions, although I haven`t seen stndDefined being much popular in ONAP/Honolulu release.

We can ask the community in the next DCAE weekly meeting, and if no-one objects, we can remove teh older SA88-rel16 files.

> > Damian - Thanks for clarification. One question - as these updates on same rls 16, can the old files (sa88-rel16) be removed? Trying to understand the need for supporting both versions.
> 
> From my perspective, we can remove the older SA88-rel16 files, this is perfectly fine with me.
> Still, they might be people, who built something around the oler SA88-rel16 descritpions, although I haven`t seen stndDefined being much popular in ONAP/Honolulu release.
> 
> We can ask the community in the next DCAE weekly meeting, and if no-one objects, we can remove teh older SA88-rel16 files.

Agreed, there was not many usecases utilizing the stdDefined domain.. Lets confirm with Mark/Oskar from Ericsson team and if no objections, we can remove older files.
